text.title: AI-Generated IaC Review Gate Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of ai-generated-iac-review-gate enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Every AI-assisted IaC PR runs the automated gate: secrets scan, policy-as-code, plan-diff, drift detection.

rationale.source: OWASP AI Code Generation review guide 2026

rule.rationale: AI tools leak secrets and produce drift that humans miss in review.

rule.statement: Blast-radius is classified before review starts; the classification gates which human reviewers are required.
rule.rationale: Without explicit classification, all changes get the same review (or none).

rule.statement: High-blast changes require 2 reviewers: senior engineer + security; both sign-offs recorded in the PR.

rationale.source: OpenSSF Secure Supply Chain Consumption Framework 2026

rule.rationale: Single-reviewer high-blast = single point of failure.

rule.statement: AI-generated comments + commit messages are reviewed for fabricated rationale (hallucinated justifications).

rationale.source: OWASP AI Code Generation review guide 2026

rule.rationale: Hallucinated justifications mislead future maintainers; auditing them prevents the trap.

rule.statement: Approved AI-IaC patterns are pinned in a reference library; novel patterns require additional review.
rule.rationale: Approved patterns reduce review surface; novel ones surface risk.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
